# Feature: Web Expert Agent — Command Surface Expansion

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-WEX-004
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Reconcile the Web Expert Agent's command surface with what users expect, and prune commands that have no real value. Today there is a mismatch between commands documented in the prompt (`/help`, `/rfc <number>`, `/trending`) and commands actually wired in `COMMAND_FOCUS` ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:190-199](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L190-L199)) — the documented ones do nothing because they're missing from the routing map. There is also redundancy: `/http`, `/ws`, `/network`, `/crypto` are narrow topic shortcuts that overlap with `/protocols`, `/security`, `/standards` and offer the user no benefit a free-text query wouldn't.

This feature:

1. **Adds the missing commands** — `/help`, `/rfc <n>`, `/trending`, `/status <code>`, `/header <name>`, `/method <verb>`, `/eli5`, `/deep`.
2. **Removes the redundant narrow commands** — `/http`, `/ws`, `/network`, `/crypto`.
3. **Special-cases commands that need argument extraction** — `/rfc <n>`, `/status <code>`, `/header <name>`, `/method <verb>` parse arguments and inject targeted context; `/trending` triggers a Hacker News fetch.
4. **Removes the dead `'local'` mode** from the public API (`getModes()`, `WebExpertMode` type) since vector-store retrieval is deferred (TODO entry already exists). `'web'` and `'auto'` remain.
5. **Extends focus strings to dictate response style as well as topic** — e.g., `/eli5` focus tells the model to use the Quick depth tier (defined in FEAT-FP-WEX-002) and skip RFC citations.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (TypeScript strict, naming, async/await, semicolons, Vitest+RTL) are in [.spec-lite/memory.md](.spec-lite/memory.md). Only spec-specific notes below.

### Approach

- All edits land in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts) and the inlined prompt constant established by FEAT-FP-WEX-001.
- **`COMMAND_FOCUS` map** ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:190-199](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L190-L199)) — remove the dropped commands, add the new no-arg commands (`/help`, `/eli5`, `/deep`). Each focus string instructs **both** topic focus AND response-style/depth.
- **`routeNode`** ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:205-228](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L205-L228)) — extend to handle argument-bearing commands (`/rfc <n>`, `/status <code>`, `/header <name>`, `/method <verb>`, `/trending`). Each special-case extracts the argument, replaces the message text with a normalized version, and adds a context entry that primes retrieval.
- **`retrieveNode`** ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:273-313](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L273-L313)) — already auto-detects `RFC_PATTERN`. Add:
  - For `/status <code>`: fetch the relevant section of RFC 9110 (HTTP semantics) via `webFetcher.fetchRfc('9110')` with the section anchor.
  - For `/header <name>`: search reference websites with the header name plus category `['http', 'web']`.
  - For `/method <verb>`: same as `/header`, with category `['http', 'standards']`.
  - For `/trending`: call a new `webFetcher.fetchUrl('https://news.ycombinator.com/')` (or extend the fetcher to expose this — the simplest path is a new method `fetchTrending()` that returns the HN front page text).
- **`/help`** — produces a static help message via the focus string ("Reply with the list of commands and what each does, then stop"). No retrieval needed.
- **`WebExpertMode` type** (in [packages/arena/src/types.ts](packages/arena/src/types.ts) or wherever it lives) — change from `'web' | 'local' | 'auto'` to `'web' | 'auto'`. Update `getModes()` ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:521-523](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L521-L523)) to return `['web', 'auto']`. Update `retrieveNode`'s `state.mode === 'local'` branch ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:308-310](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L308-L310)) — delete the TODO branch entirely.
- **Prompt-side `## Commands` table** — update the inlined prompt's commands table to reflect the new surface (drop `/http`, `/ws`, `/network`, `/crypto`; add `/status`, `/header`, `/method`, `/eli5`, `/deep`).
- **`webFetcher`** — verify whether `fetchTrending()` already exists or needs adding; if it doesn't, this feature adds a minimal method to [packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts](packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts).

### Spec-Specific Overrides

No overrides — see memory.

### Out of Scope

- Adding semantic / NLP-based command detection. Commands are exact prefix matches only.
- Building rich UI surfaces for commands (autocomplete, picker). This is agent-internal routing; UI work is the consumer's concern.
- Implementing the LLM-as-tool fetcher loop already tracked in TODO ("Let the web expert agent invoke `webFetcher.fetch()` as an LLM tool").
- Vector-store / `'local'` mode implementation (existing TODO).
- Wave-Client-specific commands (deferred to separate agent).

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

N/A — no persistent data model changes.

### Type Changes

- **`WebExpertMode`**: `'web' | 'local' | 'auto'` → `'web' | 'auto'`. Breaking type-level change for any consumer importing it; per memory General §3, backwards compatibility not required. Sweep the monorepo for usages and update.

## 4. Files

- `packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts` — `COMMAND_FOCUS` map updates, `routeNode` argument-extraction logic, `retrieveNode` mode-branch cleanup and special-case retrieval, `getModes()` update, prompt commands-table update.
- `packages/arena/src/types.ts` (or wherever `WebExpertMode` is exported — likely [packages/arena/src/types.ts](packages/arena/src/types.ts)) — narrow the union.
- `packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts` — add `fetchTrending()` method if it doesn't exist; verify `fetchRfc()` accepts a section anchor or extend it.
- All consumer files importing `WebExpertMode` or passing `mode: 'local'` — sweep and update.
- `packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts` — add tests for each new command and for the removed commands (assert `'/http'`, `'/ws'`, etc. are NOT in `COMMAND_FOCUS`).

## 5. Dependencies

- **FEAT-FP-WEX-001** (Inline System Prompt) — must ship first so the prompt-side commands-table edit lands.
- **FEAT-FP-WEX-002** (Persona & Response Structure) — strongly recommended. The `/eli5` and `/deep` focus strings reference depth tiers defined in FEAT-FP-WEX-002. If FEAT-FP-WEX-002 has not shipped, the focus strings can describe the desired depth inline as a fallback.

### TASK-002: Update `COMMAND_FOCUS` — remove redundant entries, add no-arg commands

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Edit `COMMAND_FOCUS` at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:190-199](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L190-L199):
    - **Remove**: `/http`, `/ws`, `/network`, `/crypto`.
    - **Keep**: `/protocols`, `/security`, `/standards`, `/api`.
    - **Add (no-arg)**:
      - `/help` → focus string: `"Respond with the complete commands list and what each does, then stop. Use the GFM table from the prompt's ## Commands section verbatim. Do not answer any other question in the same response."`
      - `/eli5` → focus string: `"Use the Quick depth tier per ## Audience & Depth Tiers. Plain English, no jargon, one analogy if helpful, skip RFC citations."`
      - `/deep` → focus string: `"Use the Deep depth tier per ## Audience & Depth Tiers. Walk through the spec section by section, include edge cases, comparison tables, and multiple examples."`
  - Tighten existing `/protocols`, `/security`, `/standards`, `/api` focus strings to also specify response depth (default to Default tier unless the user has otherwise indicated).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none in code (commands table in the prompt is updated in TASK-004).
- **Verify**: build succeeds; routing tests in TASK-005 confirm new entries match.

### TASK-003: Extend `routeNode` to handle argument-bearing commands and `/trending`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `routeNode` at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:205-228](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L205-L228), add prefix handling **before** the existing `COMMAND_FOCUS` loop for these commands (each path strips the prefix, normalizes the message, and adds a context-focus hint):
    - **`/rfc <n>`**: extract digits with regex `^\/rfc\s+(\d{1,5})\b`. Replace message with `"Explain RFC <n>: cite the abstract, key sections, and relationships to other RFCs."` and add context entry `"Focus on RFC <n>. Cite section numbers."`. The existing `RFC_PATTERN` in `retrieveNode` will then auto-fetch.
    - **`/status <code>`**: extract three-digit code with regex `^\/status\s+([1-5]\d{2})\b`. Validate code is in 100–599 range. Replace message with `"Explain HTTP status code <code>: meaning, RFC 9110 section, and typical use cases."` and add context entry `"Focus on HTTP status code <code> per RFC 9110."`. Add `'9110'` to a list `state.context` later picks up for fetch (see TASK-004).
    - **`/header <name>`**: extract header name with regex `^\/header\s+([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]*)\b`. Replace message with `"Explain the HTTP header '<name>': purpose, syntax, RFC reference, and example values."` and add context entry `"Focus on HTTP header <name>. Prefer RFC 9110/9111/9112/9113/9114 and MDN."`.
    - **`/method <verb>`**: extract verb with regex `^\/method\s+([A-Z]+)\b`. Replace message with `"Explain the HTTP method <verb>: semantics, safety, idempotency, and RFC 9110 section."` and add context entry `"Focus on HTTP method <verb> per RFC 9110 §9.3."`.
    - **`/trending`**: replace message with `"Summarize the current trending web/dev topics from Hacker News."` and add context entry `"Source: Hacker News front page. Trigger fetch."`. Mark with a sentinel field on returned state (e.g., `sources: ['hn:trending']`) so `retrieveNode` knows to call `fetchTrending()`.
  - Keep the existing no-arg `COMMAND_FOCUS` loop afterward as a fallback for commands without arguments.
  - For invalid argument formats (e.g., `/status abc`, `/rfc xyz`), fall through with a focus message indicating the syntax error: `"User invoked <prefix> with an invalid argument. Reply with the correct syntax and a one-line example."`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: add a JSDoc block above the new prefix-handling code listing all special-cased commands.
- **Verify**: build succeeds; routing tests in TASK-005 confirm each command produces the expected normalized message and context entries.

### TASK-004: Extend `retrieveNode` and `webFetcher` for `/status` and `/trending`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In [packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts](packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts):
    - Add a method `fetchTrending(): Promise<{ title: string; content: string; url: string }>` that fetches `https://news.ycombinator.com/` and extracts the top-N story titles + URLs into a markdown summary. Reuse the existing fetch + parse infrastructure already used by `search()`.
    - Verify `fetchRfc()` accepts a section anchor parameter; if not, extend its signature to `fetchRfc(rfcNumber: string, section?: string)` and append `#section-<n>` (or the appropriate anchor format) to the URL when provided.
  - In `retrieveNode` at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:273-313](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L273-L313):
    - Detect the sentinel context entry `"Focus on HTTP status code <code> per RFC 9110."` (or use a structured marker passed via state — preferred). When detected, call `webFetcher.fetchRfc('9110', '<status-section>')` to retrieve the RFC 9110 section covering that status family.
    - Detect the sentinel `sources: ['hn:trending']` from `routeNode`. When present, call `webFetcher.fetchTrending()` and append the result to `context` and `sources`.
  - Prefer a structured state field over string-sniffing — extend `WebExpertAgentState` with an optional `commandHints?: { kind: 'status' | 'trending' | ...; arg?: string }` field so the routing → retrieval handoff is type-safe.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005, plus a unit test for `webFetcher.fetchTrending()` in [packages/arena/src/test/tools/webFetcher.test.ts](packages/arena/src/test/tools/webFetcher.test.ts) (create if it doesn't exist) that mocks `fetch` to return a stub HN HTML page and asserts the extracted titles.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `fetchTrending()` and any new `fetchRfc()` parameter.
- **Verify**: build succeeds; new fetcher unit test passes; integration tests in TASK-005 confirm context is populated for `/status` and `/trending`.
- **Depends on**: TASK-003.

The mock LLM should receive the original message intact (i.e., the prefix is treated as ordinary text now). Capture and assert.

  - **Test 5 — `/status 429`**: invoke, assert the user message is normalized, the context references RFC 9110, and `webFetcher.fetchRfc` was called with `'9110'` (and a section anchor if implemented).

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: New fetcher methods (`fetchTrending`, optional section anchor on `fetchRfc`) follow the existing fetcher's error pattern — wrap in `try/catch`, log via `console.warn` (matching [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:291](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L291) and [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:304](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L304)), continue retrieval without the failed source.
- **Logging**: Existing `console.info`/`console.warn` pattern in `routeNode`/`retrieveNode` is preserved. Add a `console.info` in `routeNode` when a special-cased command matches (e.g., `'[WebExpert/route] command matched: /rfc 9110'`) so the existing log-tail script (memory Logging §1) shows command activity.
- **Security**: `/trending` fetches a public URL with no credentials. `/header <name>`, `/status <code>`, `/method <verb>` argument extraction uses anchored regexes that reject anything other than the expected character classes — no injection risk into downstream fetch URLs (and the fetcher already has a per-domain rate limiter and reference-website allowlist).
- **Performance**: New commands trigger one extra fetch per matched command. The existing rate limiter ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:128](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L128)) caps per-domain QPS. No hot-path concerns.
- **Backwards compatibility**: Per memory General §3, not required. Removing `/http`, `/ws`, `/network`, `/crypto` and the `'local'` mode is a clean break.
